summary:
The aggressive use of crotalid polyvalent antivenoms can lead to significant acute side effects including severe allergic reactions or serum sickness affecting up to 13%, despite improved safety profiles over older versions. These events may require medical intervention and could worsen underlying conditions like coagulopathy or angioedema, highlighting ongoing risks regardless of formulation improvements. 

Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically pertinent information related directly to acute adverse effects from using these treatments aggressively.
